mappings.rationaleAI: Ageing covers biological mechanisms of senescence, telomere shortening, evolutionary theories of aging, life extension questions, and age-related diseases -- directly relevant to aging and dying/death stages of the human life cycle.
Immunisation covers the immune system's mechanisms, active and passive immunisation, and vaccine development -- directly relevant to the immune response as a defense against disease.

Circadian rhythms govern sleep-wake patterns, with coverage of the suprachiasmatic nucleus as the master clock, REM/NREM cycles, and disruptions like jet lag -- directly relevant to the state of sleep and psychophysiological variations in sleep.

Free radicals covers cellular damage, the free radical theory of aging, antioxidant defense mechanisms, and the role of oxidative stress in disease -- relevant to both maintenance of internal environment under stress and aging.

Hormones covers adrenaline and the stress response regulated by the hypothalamus-pituitary axis -- directly relevant to the alarm reaction (preparation through hormonal effects for flight or resistance).

Mitochondrial dysfunction's role in ageing and disease, and mitochondrial involvement in apoptosis, is relevant to the maintenance of the internal environment and adaptation of cells to severe stress.
Pasteur's germ theory and vaccine development are foundational to understanding bodily defenses against disease including inflammation and the immune response.

Man and Disease covers the history of disease understanding from humoral medicine through germ theory, vaccination, antibiotics, and public health -- relevant to defenses against disease and healing processes.
